visa_types.name: Re-entry Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Checklist for Re-entry Visa
- Visa application form, filled in completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) +1 photocopy of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- In the case of a theft or a loss of residence permit, a copy of the report of loss or theft of the local police force translated into Italian
- In the case of the expiry of a residence permit within a time lower than 60 days, the original or the copy of the expired document and a copy of the receipt of request for renewal
Clarification  on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at  least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence
                                                                            of both parents, by a person in possession of a notorized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;
- In case of family reunión application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the
                                                                            issuance of the authorization(Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office(via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admisible and therefore not accepted.The principles
                                                                        referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
People lawfully residing in Italy, having lost their residence permit or people having left the country before receiving the renewal of the said permit, can apply for the re-entry visa
                                                                        proving that he/she has travelled out of the country for genuine and importatnt reasons.
The visa will be issued from the competent "Questura" (Police Headquarters) in Italy. It is a long term period visa (type D) - Single entry.
visa_types.full_text:

People lawfully residing in Italy, having lost their residence permit or people having left the country before receiving the renewal of the said permit, can apply for the re-entry visa

The visa will be issued from the competent "Questura" (Police Headquarters) in Italy. It is a long term period visa (type D) - Single entry.

ACCRA – 31 January 2017

                                                                            of both parents, by a person in possession of a notorized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;

                                                                            issuance of the authorization(Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office(via E-mail);

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

 Standard processing time is 15 calendar days.

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Visa Information:
 A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Italy Visa Application
                                                                Centre in Ghana. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply to the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent
                                                                in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the embassy of the country which will be your first point of entry. If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at  the Italy Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period  of stay in the Schengen area must be mainly in Italy. Please provide proof to  support this information.
visa_types.full_text:
## Schengen Visa Information:
 A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Italy Visa Application
                                                                Centre in Ghana. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply to the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent
                                                                in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the embassy of the country which will be your first point of entry. If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at  the Italy Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period  of stay in the Schengen area must be mainly in Italy. Please provide proof to  support this information.

## Required Travel Insurance
 A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Italy and other Schengen states. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euros and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization and
                                                                repatriation due to injury or medical reasons.  The reimbursement from the insurance company must be payable in any EU-country, Switzerland and Lichtenstein.  The company issuing the insurance can be based anywhere but preferably you should use a company base in your home country or the country you are travelling to. The Embassy must asses
                                                                whether the company is solid or not. For this purpose all Schengen Missions in Ghana have compiled a list of trusted companies. If you use a company that has not been assessed by the Schengen
                                                                Missions the handling time of your application will be at least 30 days.  Please note that you have to bring your travel insurance with you when travelling due to inspections of relevant documents at the border control when entering the Schengen area.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is issued to applicants to allow them to enter Italy for a short term period of stay, for not more than 90 days and is issued to foreigners who have an economical commercial
                                                                        purpose.

## Visa Fees

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Business (including Fairs and Conferences) > Visa Fees 1]
2. Service Charges 

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Business (including Fairs and Conferences) > Visa Fees 2]
(The fee change is as per the currency exchange rates)
The above-mentioned service charges include VAT 12.5% and NHIL 2.5%. Fee amount can be paid only in local currency.
The fee will be applicable per set of documents to be legalized (each set will contain a maximum of 4 documents, (i.e) 2 originals and 2 translations). From the fifth document, the
                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Business (including Fairs and Conferences)
- Visa application form, filled in completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) +1 photocopy of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Introduction letter from employer or school + parental consent (if applicant under 18 years)
- International health insurance cover for the duration of stay
- Flight booking slip - Reservation of the bill return ticket or bill or proof of the availability of personal means of transport
- Air-ticket (open) for only applicants of long stay visa + 1 photocopy
Proof of economic ties with  Ghana (+1 photocopy)
- Payslips of the last 3 months
- Employment letter stating terms and monthly income
- Letter from employer
- Bank statements of the past 3 months
- Business papers (if self-employed)
Proof of social ties (+1 photocopy)
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Family record certificate
- Official leave letter from the university or school (if applicable)
Business
The following original documents (+1 Photocopy) must be produced
- Evidence of the registered business in Ghana (original certificates including:
Form  C, Form 3 and Companies Code
- VAT Registration, tax clearance certificate and bills of lading
- Invitation, pro-forma invoice,
- certificate of business registration (with Italian Chamber of Commerce)
- Original correspondence with your business contact in Italy
- Original letter indicating designation and authority to act on behalf of company
- Bank statements (personal and company) for last 3 months (+1 photocopy)
- Reservation for accommodation (Hotel Reservation)
- Recent copy of the Register of Trade (« Visura camerale ») from the Italian company
Clarification  on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at  least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence
                                                                            of both parents, by a person in possession of a notorized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;
- In case of family reunión application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the
                                                                            issuance of the authorization(Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office(via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admisible and therefore not accepted.The principles
                                                                        referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 Standard processing time is 15 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Please find the Visa/Legalization application form below.

The forms are stored in the Adobe Portable Document format (PDF). To read them on screen and print them out you need to have the Adobe Acrobat Reader programme installed on your computer.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Visa allows applicants to enter Italy for a short term period (type "C") or for a long term period (type "D") with the purpose to work while earning a salary.
This Visas are subject to an Authorisation ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authority ("SPORTELLO UNICO PER L'IMMIGRAZIONE") which is valid within 6 months from the date
                                                                        of issuing.

## Visa Fees

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Subordinate Employment Visa > Visa Fees 1]
2. Service Charges 

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Subordinate Employment Visa > Visa Fees 2]
(The fee change is as per the currency exchange rates)
The above-mentioned service charges include VAT 12.5% and NHIL 2.5%. Fee amount can be paid only in local currency.
(*) The fee will be applicable per set of documents to be legalized (each set will contain a maximum of 4 documents, (i.e) 2 originals and 2 translations). From the fifth document, the
                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Subordinate Employment Visa
- Visa application form, filled in completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) +1 photocopy of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Birth Certificate legalized by the Ministry for Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Ghana.
- Copy of "Nulla Osta" delivered since less than 6 months by "Sportello Unico per l’Immigrazione".
- Contract of Employment
Clarification  on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at  least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence
                                                                            of both parents, by a person in possession of a notorized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;
- In case of family reunión application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the
                                                                            issuance of the authorization(Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office(via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admisible and therefore not accepted.The principles
                                                                        referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 Standard processing time is 15 calendar days.

                                                                        proving that he/she has travelled out of the country for genuine and important reasons.

## Overview
The visa is issued to people who wish to travel to Italy for Transit purposes. It is a short term (type "C") visa

## Visa Fees

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Transit Visa > Visa Fees 1]
2. Service Charges 

[ita > tgo > en > Table:Transit Visa > Visa Fees 2]
(The fee change is as per the currency exchange rates)
The above-mentioned service charges include VAT 12.5% and NHIL 2.5%. Fee amount can be paid only in local currency.
(*) The fee will be applicable per set of documents to be legalized (each set will contain a maximum of 4 documents, (i.e) 2 originals and 2 translations). From the fifth document, the
                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
- Checklist for Transit Visa
- Visa application form, filled in completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) +1 photocopy of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Flight booking slip - proof of the availability of personal means of transport
- Documentation showing the reason of travelling and proving the necessity to transit through Italy, especially LOI (Letter of Invitation) from Employer, employment contract and any other relevant documents pertaining to employment on vessel.
- Additional Documents if present
- Seaman’s booklet
- Certifications of maritime courses taken, original and copy

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 Standard processing time is 15 calendar days.

- Previous passports, (if any) +1 photocopy of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Introduction letter from employer or school + parental consent (if applicant under 18 years)
- International health insurance cover for the duration of stay
- Flight booking slip - Reservation of the bill return ticket or bill or proof of the availability of personal means of transport
- Air-ticket (open) for only applicants of long stay visa + 1 photocopy
Proof of    economic ties with Ghana (+1 photocopy)
- Payslips of the last 3 months
- Employment letter stating terms and monthly income
- Letter from employer
- Bank statements of the past 3 months
- - Business papers (if self-employed)
Proof of    social ties (+1 photocopy)
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Family record certificate
- Official leave letter from the university or school (if applicable)
Business
- The following original documents (+1 Photocopy) must be produced
- Evidence of the registered business in Ghana (original certificates including:
Form    C, Form 3 and Companies Code
- VAT Registration, tax clearance certificate and bills of lading
- Invitation, pro-forma invoice,
- certificate of business registration (with Italian Chamber of Commerce)
- Original correspondence with your business contact in Italy
- Original letter indicating designation and authority to act on behalf of company
- Bank statements (personal and company) for last 3 months (+1 photocopy)
- Reservation for accommodation (Hotel Reservation)
- Recent copy of the Register of Trade (« Visura camerale ») from the Italian company
Clarification  on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at  least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence
                                                                            of both parents, by a person in possession of a notorized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;
- In case of family reunión application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the
                                                                            issuance of the authorization(Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office(via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admisible and therefore not accepted.The principles
                                                                        referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 Standard processing time is 15 calendar days
